summary: Guest-side reversible probe on Win25H2Clean confirmed that HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Beep\Start = 4 disables the Beep driver on this build. Microsoft service registry documentation defines Start = 4 as Disabled, so the app's write now has a machine-checkable contract.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: lines 52-58: The `Start` value specifies when the service should be started. `0x4` (Disabled): The service is disabled and will not be loaded.
validation_proof.notes: Guest reversible probe at H:\Temp\vm-tooling-staging\beep_start_toggle_out.txt confirmed BASELINE=1, AFTER_4=4, AFTER_1=1, RESTORED=1.

setting.name: System Beep Driver
setting.casual_explanation: This affects the old system beep behavior driven by the Beep driver. Most people will never notice it unless they are cleaning up legacy system sounds or debugging old compatibility behavior.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it when they want to suppress the legacy hardware-style beep or strip down older sound behaviors.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Disabling the Beep driver can remove legacy beep behavior, but it also removes the audible cue some old components may still expect.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft service registry documentation defines the Start value for services and drivers. The guest probe on Win25H2Clean confirmed that Start=4 disables the Beep driver and that the observed baseline on this build is Start=1.

allowed_values.label: System start (observed baseline)
allowed_values.meaning: Loaded by the I/O subsystem. The guest probe restored this value as the baseline on Win25H2Clean.

allowed_values.label: Disabled
allowed_values.meaning: The service is disabled and will not be loaded. The current app writes 4 here to disable the Beep driver.

targets.notes: Start=4 disables the Beep driver on Win25H2Clean. The observed baseline on this build was Start=1.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes Start = 4 for the Beep driver, and the guest probe confirmed that Start=4 disables the Beep driver on Win25H2Clean.

windows_defaults.label: Windows service baseline observed in guest
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows systems exposing the Beep driver key

states.rationale: Guest baseline on Win25H2Clean was Start=1 and the reversible probe restored it to that value.

windows_defaults.notes: The observed baseline on Win25H2Clean was Start=1.

recommended_profiles.label: Turn off Beep Driver
intended_for: People who want to suppress legacy system beep behavior
Users cleaning up old audio cues
avoid_for: Systems that still rely on the legacy beep cue

states.rationale: Start=4 disables the Beep driver and will not load it.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to suppress the legacy Beep driver entirely.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need the legacy beep cue to remain available.
tradeoffs: Reversible by writing Start=1 and restoring the baseline.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows managed default
intended_for: Users who want the Beep driver left at its observed baseline

states.rationale: Leave the driver at the observed baseline value.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You do not want to disable the Beep driver.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to suppress legacy beep behavior.
tradeoffs: Beep remains enabled at the observed baseline state.

evidence.title: HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services Registry Tree
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents service and driver Start values, including 0x4 (Disabled) meaning the service is disabled and will not be loaded.

evidence.title: Guest reversible probe - Beep driver start mode
evidence.summary: Guest reversible probe on Win25H2Clean confirmed Start transitions through 1 and 4 before restoring to 1.

evidence.title: Repo source note for audio.disable-beep
evidence.summary: The repo source note links the Beep start value to upstream nohuto research and marks the tweak as repo-backed.

decision.why: The Microsoft service registry documentation defines Start=4 as Disabled, and the guest-side reversible probe on Win25H2Clean confirmed that the current app write matches that contract.
